steps.title: Calculate calcium from milk
steps.content:
Each cup of milk has 286 mg of calcium. The batch uses 4 cups, so the total calcium from milk is:
$$4 \times 286 = 1144 \text{ mg}$$

steps.title: Find calcium from bananas
steps.content:
The batch contains 1,158 mg total. The calcium from bananas is:
$$1158 - 1144 = 14 \text{ mg}$$
This is from 2 bananas combined.

steps.title: Divide by number of bananas
steps.content:
Dividing by the number of bananas:
$$\frac{14}{2} = 7 \text{ mg per banana}$$
That matches Choice D — 7.
